summary:
Phenylbutyrate is effective but limited by side effects like toxicity; essential yet requires cautious management due to adverse reactions and dietary considerations. Sodium phenylacetate also plays a crucial role in reducing ammonia levels through alternative pathways when combined with other treatments. To address your query directly about drugs specifically aimed at lowering blood ammonia concentrations in patients suffering from UCDs, both agents are pivotal components often prescribed together under medical supervision considering their efficacy balanced against safety concerns.
